aliases: Organization of the Base in the Arabian Peninsula
description: Sunni Islamist militant organization seeking to overthrow the Yemeni government and establish an Islamic emirate. Formed in 2009 from the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia. Based in Yemen, it is the most active al-Qaeda affiliate, conducting attacks in Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and the West. Its strength has waned due to internal struggles, civil war, and US drone strikes.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 7 January 2015, French-Algerian brothers Saïd and Chérif Kouachi attacked the Paris headquarters of French satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo... On 14 January 2015, AQAP claimed responsibility for the Charlie Hebdo shooting...
country_links.context: AQAP claimed responsibility for the Charlie Hebdo shooting in Paris.

country_links.evidence_quote: ...to Schiphol Airport in Amsterdam, Netherlands, before transferring to Northwest Airlines Flight 253.
country_links.context: AQAP's operative transited through Amsterdam en route to the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Abdulmutallab travelled from Murtala Muhammed International Airport in Lagos, Nigeria to Schiphol Airport in Amsterdam, Netherlands...
country_links.context: AQAP recruited Umar Farouk Abdulmutallab in Nigeria and he traveled from Lagos to conduct the bombing attempt.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 27 August 2009, a member of AQAP, Abdallah Al-Asiri, attempted to assassinate the Prince Muhammad bin Nayef, a Senior Member of Saudi Royal family... American and Saudi intelligence agencies foiled an AQAP plot to smuggle a bomb onto a plane bound to the United States on 8 May 2012.
country_links.context: AQAP attempted to assassinate a Saudi prince and Saudi intelligence foiled a bomb plot.

country_links.evidence_quote: Emirati authorities in Dubai subsequently discovered another printer bomb inside of a FedEx Express cargo plane.
country_links.context: AQAP sent a cargo bomb that was discovered in Dubai.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 29 October 2010, British police at East Midlands Airport defused a printer cartridge bomb loaded on a UPS cargo plane after a tip from Saudi Arabian intelligence...
country_links.context: AQAP sent a cargo bomb that was discovered at East Midlands Airport.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 25 December 2009, Nigerian foreign student Umar Farouk Abdulmutallab attempted to bomb Northwest Airlines Flight 253 while it prepared to land in Detroit, Michigan... AQAP took responsibility for the attempted bombing... On 29 October 2010... two packages were bound to out of date addresses for Jewish institutions in Chicago... On 6 December 2019, Saudi Arabian Mohammed Saeed Alshamrani attacked Naval Air Station Pensacola in Pensacola, Florida... AQAP released a video claiming responsibility for the shooting.
country_links.context: AQAP conducted the attempted bombing of Northwest Airlines Flight 253 over Detroit, the cargo plane bomb plot targeting Chicago, and the Pensacola shooting in Florida.
